free_text: The neutron source was a 1/2" thick Be target
Metal used in the experiments were usually machined
cylinders and were measured by two different
observers with micrometers or vernier calipers. The
liquids employed were weighed in a 500-ml volumetric
flask. Chemically pure materials were used.

The inelastic cross-section measurements are subject
to greater sources of error. The "poor geometry"
detector count inelastically scattered neutrons or
neutrons ejected from nuclei if their energies are
above the threshold for bismuth fission. The
represent a lower limit to the true values.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
